title: Control Flow Nodes (Switch, IndexSwitch, RepeatInput/Output, GroupInput/Output)
description: Implement 6 control flow nodes that enable conditional and iterative behavior in node graphs. Switch: select between two inputs based on a boolean. IndexSwitch: select from N inputs based on an integer index. RepeatInput/RepeatOutput: loop a subgraph N times, passing output of iteration i as input to iteration i+1. NodeGroupInput/NodeGroupOutput: define the interface of a node group (exposed parameters in, results out). These depend on the evaluation engine since they affect graph traversal order.
definition-of-done: 1. Six node classes in nodes/control/. 2. Switch correctly passes through input A or B based on boolean. 3. IndexSwitch handles N inputs with out-of-range clamping. 4. RepeatInput/Output loops the enclosed subgraph N times with state forwarding. 5. GroupInput/GroupOutput define exposed parameters and results for node groups. 6. The evaluation engine correctly handles these nodes' special traversal requirements.
testing-plan: 1. Switch: wire two cubes, toggle boolean, verify correct mesh passes through. 2. IndexSwitch: wire 3 meshes, set index to each, verify. 3. Repeat: subdivide a cube 3 times via repeat, verify face count = 12 * 4^3. 4. GroupInput/Output: create a 'scale cube' group with size parameter, invoke with different sizes.
todos: Implement SwitchNode with boolean condition and two generic inputs
Implement IndexSwitchNode with integer index and N inputs
Implement RepeatInputNode and RepeatOutputNode as a loop pair
Integrate repeat loop execution into NodeGraph evaluator
Implement GroupInputNode and GroupOutputNode for node group interfaces
Add special-case handling in the evaluation engine for control flow nodes
unknowns: Whether Repeat should have a maximum iteration cap to prevent infinite loops
How GroupInput/Output integrate with the node group system (MESH-14) -- may need co-development
